name: Amman
summary: Amman is generally considered one of the safest capitals in the Middle East under normal circumstances, but current regional tensions and U.S. State Department Level 3 travel advisory significantly impact safety considerations. The city typically experiences low violent crime rates and moderate petty crime, though current geopolitical factors require extra caution.
quickVerdict: While Amman has historically been a safe destination with low crime rates and welcoming locals, current regional conflicts and security concerns have prompted a U.S. State Department Level 3 advisory (Reconsider Travel). The evacuation of non-emergency U.S. personnel in March 2026 due to terrorism and armed conflict risks means travelers should seriously consider postponing visits. For those who choose to travel despite advisories, standard precautions against petty crime, tourist scams, and cultural sensitivity remain important, but regional security takes precedence.

neighborhoods.name: Jabal Amman/Rainbow Street
neighborhoods.description: Historic upscale area with embassies, hotels, restaurants and Rainbow Street. Well-patrolled and generally safe for tourists, though avoid large gatherings during current tensions.

neighborhoods.name: Jabal al-Weibdeh
neighborhoods.description: Bohemian artistic quarter with galleries, cafes, and cultural sites. Very safe for walking day and night, quiet atmosphere, popular with expats and creative community.

neighborhoods.name: Abdoun
neighborhoods.description: Affluent residential area with upscale shopping, restaurants, and embassies. Considered the safest and most secure neighborhood in Amman with good infrastructure.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown (Al-Balad)
neighborhoods.description: Historic city center with Roman sites and traditional souks. Crowded and chaotic with higher pickpocketing risk. Exercise extra caution during current regional tensions.

neighborhoods.name: Sweifieh/Shmeisani
neighborhoods.description: Modern business district with shopping malls, hotels, and restaurants. Generally safe but can be crowded. Good transport connections throughout the city.

neighborhoods.name: East Amman
neighborhoods.description: Working-class area with higher poverty levels. Less tourist infrastructure, potential for demonstrations, and generally not recommended for visitors especially during current security climate.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Drivers quote inflated prices or refuse to use meters, especially with foreign tourists. Some claim meters are broken or quote fixed prices for short trips.
scams.howToAvoid: Always insist on the meter (say 'il adad' in Arabic). Use Uber/Careem when available. Agree on price beforehand for longer trips and ask your hotel for typical fare estimates.

scams.name: Bedouin Romance Scam
scams.description: Targeting solo female travelers in Petra and Wadi Rum, locals offer authentic experiences, companionship, or romantic interest to extract money or inappropriate situations.
scams.howToAvoid: Be cautious of overly friendly approaches from tour guides or locals. Book official tours through licensed operators. Don't accept invitations to sleep in caves or isolated locations.

scams.name: Aggressive Tipping Culture
scams.description: Excessive tipping expectations for every small service, sometimes becoming pushy or aggressive. Can feel like harassment rather than genuine service.
scams.howToAvoid: Learn local tipping standards (10% restaurants, small amounts for services). Don't feel obligated to tip for unwanted help. Politely refuse services you don't need.

scams.name: Tourist Souvenir Markup
scams.description: Vendors in tourist areas significantly inflate prices for souvenirs, claiming items are handmade when mass-produced, or using high-pressure sales tactics.
scams.howToAvoid: Research typical prices beforehand. Practice bargaining skills - start at 50% of asking price. Shop at fixed-price stores for comparison. Don't buy on impulse.

soloFemale.overview: Jordan is generally considered one of the more female-friendly destinations in the Middle East, though current regional tensions add security concerns. Many solo female travelers report positive experiences, feeling safer than in neighboring countries. However, unwanted male attention, staring, and cultural differences around gender roles are common issues. Conservative dress and cultural awareness significantly improve experiences.
tips: Dress modestly covering shoulders and knees - this reduces unwanted attention significantly
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in safe neighborhoods like Jabal Amman or Weibdeh
Use Uber/Careem or reputable taxis rather than walking alone at night
Join group tours for major attractions to avoid being singled out
Consider traveling with other female travelers met at hostels or through travel groups
Be aware of current security situation and avoid demonstrations or large gatherings

nightSafety.overview: Amman's nightlife safety varies significantly by area and current security conditions. Upscale neighborhoods like Abdoun and Jabal Amman are generally safe for evening dining and socializing. However, current regional tensions mean extra caution is advised after dark, with recommendations to stay in well-lit, populated areas and avoid late night street walking.
tips: Stick to well-lit main streets and avoid walking alone after 10pm
Use Uber/Careem for night transportation rather than walking or street taxis
Stay in upscale areas like Rainbow Street or Abdoun for evening entertainment
Inform accommodation staff of your plans when going out at night
Avoid large gatherings or demonstrations which can occur without warning
Keep embassy contact information readily available given current security climate

transport.metro: No metro system exists in Amman. Public buses connect main areas but are infrequent and can be confusing for tourists.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Careem are widely available and recommended as the safest transport option. Both apps work well with English interface, transparent pricing, and driver tracking features.
transport.taxis: Yellow taxis are ubiquitous but meter usage can be inconsistent. White service taxis follow fixed routes but are complicated for visitors. Always negotiate fares beforehand or insist on meter use.
transport.tips: Use Uber/Careem as first choice for safety and convenience. For regular taxis, always insist on meter ('il adad') and have your destination written in Arabic. Avoid shared white taxis unless familiar with routes.

customs: Dress modestly covering shoulders and knees, especially important for women and when visiting religious sites
Avoid public displays of affection between couples - even holding hands can attract unwanted attention
Remove shoes when entering homes and some traditional establishments
Use right hand for greetings, eating, and passing items - left hand is considered unclean
Show respect during call to prayer times and avoid eating/drinking in public during Ramadan daylight hours

health.overview: Jordan has good healthcare infrastructure with modern hospitals and well-trained medical staff. Amman hosts several high-quality private hospitals that serve medical tourists from across the region.
health.water: Tap water is generally safe in Amman but bottled water is recommended for sensitive stomachs. Hotels and restaurants use safe water sources.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for Jordan. Routine vaccines (MMR, DPT, flu) should be up to date. Consider Hepatitis A/B if staying longer or eating street food.
health.altitude: Amman sits at 750m elevation - no altitude concerns. Climate is Mediterranean with hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters. Sun protection essential year-round.

emergency.usEmbassy: Due to current security situation, U.S. Embassy has suspended routine services. For emergencies: +962-6-590-6500. Americans should enroll in STEP program and contact AmmanACS@state.gov for emergency assistance.

faq.a: Under normal circumstances, Amman is considered one of the safest Middle Eastern capitals. However, current U.S. State Department Level 3 advisory and evacuation of non-emergency personnel due to regional conflicts means travelers should seriously consider postponing visits until tensions decrease.

Upscale areas like Jabal Amman and Abdoun are generally safe for evening activities, but current security concerns mean extra caution is advised after dark. Use rideshare services, stick to well-lit areas, and avoid demonstrations or large gatherings that can occur without warning.

faq.q: Is Amman safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Jordan is considered more female-friendly than many regional countries, and many solo women travel successfully. However, expect unwanted attention and cultural challenges. Current security situation adds additional concerns - consider group tours or travel companions during this period.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Amman?
faq.a: East Amman neighborhoods are generally not tourist-oriented and less safe. Currently, avoid all large gatherings, demonstrations, and border areas. The U.S. Embassy area may also be avoided per security alerts about potential targeting.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Amman?
faq.a: Regular public buses exist but are not recommended for tourists due to language barriers and infrequent service. Uber and Careem are safe, convenient options. Official yellow taxis are generally safe if meters are used, though negotiate fares beforehand for longer trips.
